idea.title: Token Triage
idea.hook: Drag incoming AI requests into cheap/expensive model lanes before the budget bar empties.
idea.hook_ru: Перетягивай входящие запросы в дешёвые или дорогие модели, пока не кончился бюджет.
idea.prototype: Top-down conveyor: cards labelled SIMPLE/MEDIUM/HARD slide in from the right on a moving belt. Three lane drop zones below: Haiku (1 tk, simple-only), Sonnet (4 tk, simple+medium), Opus (9 tk, all). Drag a card from the conveyor down to a lane to route it. Correct route = +reward + streak bump; wrong lane = -12 budget and token wasted; card escaping off the left edge = -5 budget and +1 escape. Budget≤0 OR 3 escapes = GAMEOVER. Difficulty ramps every 15s: spawn interval shrinks 1800→600 ms, hard chance rises 0.15→0.45.
idea.mutation_of: Mutates 32_token_budget — the budget-as-currency frame stays, but the routing economy gains a real-time conveyor + tiered model lanes from the Anthropic-rate-hike + Codex-tariff signals on the digest (vibecoding_tg/3143, timurtaepov/1093). Conveyor sorting is a winning casual genre (gamedev-strategy), tiered cost adds the agent-cost dimension nobody else is mining yet.
idea.why_novel: First arcade where the difficulty stamp (SIMPLE/MEDIUM/HARD) is information you have to READ on a moving card, not just see — the colour band is subtle on early waves to tempt mis-reads. Tiered model lanes turn cost-optimisation into a snap decision per card. Real-world agent-cost framing reads as a wink to the audience that built it.
idea.non_timer_pressure: Budget bar (100 → 0) + escape counter (0 → 3 = game over). Conveyor speed and HARD-chance ramp every 15s, so spatial density rises without any countdown clock.

sources.title: Token Triage
sources.snippet: Entry #1 — Token Triage: drag AI requests into cheap/expensive model lanes before the budget bar empties. Conveyor sorting + tiered cost economy.

sources.title: Codex 5.5 medium beats extra-high — agent-rules signal
sources.snippet: Add this rule to Agents.md: do not fight with errors. The cheaper model with better rules can beat the more expensive one.

sources.title: Anthropic +50% weekly limits
sources.snippet: Anthropic temporarily raised weekly limits 50% through July 13 — agent budget signal in the wild.

sources.title: Claude -p moves to API tariffication
sources.snippet: Sad news: claude -p is essentially moving to API pricing. $100/mo monthly credit means background scripts and automated tools now bleed budget.

synthesizer_brief: Real-time conveyor sorter where cards = AI requests stamped SIMPLE/MEDIUM/HARD and lanes = Haiku/Sonnet/Opus with tiered token costs. Pick the cheapest lane that won't fail. Budget bar drains on mis-routes, three escapes ends the run. Difficulty ramps via conveyor speed + HARD-chance every 15s. Endless score-attack, best persists. Mutates 32_token_budget's budget-as-currency frame with a tiered-cost routing economy pulled from the day's agent-cost news (vibecoding/timurtaepov).
